auth.welcomeSub: Sign in to access the Arenet console.

errors.oidcGeneric: Sign-in failed (code: {code}). Try again, or use a local account.
errors.oidcNotAuthorized: This account is not allowed to sign in. Ask an administrator to add your e-mail or OIDC identifier to the access list.
errors.oidcInvalidState: The SSO request was rejected (invalid, expired or replayed state token). Try again.
errors.oidcIdpError: The identity provider rejected the sign-in request. Contact the IdP administrator.
errors.oidcIdpUnreachable: The identity provider is unreachable. Try again later, or sign in with a local account below.
errors.oidcInternal: An unexpected error occurred during SSO sign-in. Try again, or use a local account.

dnsProviders.title: DNS Providers
dnsProviders.subtitle: DNS accounts used to issue wildcard certificates (ACME DNS-01).

modal.secretsKeepHint: Leave blank to keep the current value.

delete.error409: Provider is in use by: {wildcards}. Reassign or remove those wildcards first.

routes.pageSubtitle: Manage reverse proxy routes — hosts, upstreams, TLS, WAF, authentication.
routes.importCaddyfileTooltip: Phase 2 — Caddyfile import not yet wired
routes.noMatchFilter: No routes match the current filter.

dns01Banner.title: DNS-01 routes need a DNS provider.
dns01Banner.message: At least one route is configured for DNS-01 ACME, but the OVH DNS provider is missing or incomplete in
dns01Banner.tail: . Certificate renewals for these routes will fail until the provider is configured.

panel.emptyHint: Select a route on the left to edit it, or click

delete.title: Delete route
delete.confirmText: Are you sure you want to delete the route for
delete.warning: Caddy will be reloaded immediately. This action cannot be undone.

wafCRSDialog.title: Disable the OWASP CRS rules?
wafCRSDialog.message: You are about to stop loading the OWASP Core Rule Set on this route. Protection against SQLi, XSS, RCE, LFI and Protocol attacks will no longer be active. Use this only for trusted internal APIs (LAN) or legacy routes generating recurring false-positives. You can undo this action at any time by ticking the checkbox back.

form.tlsEnableHelper: Public domain required for Let's Encrypt; localhost / .local will fall back to internal CA.
form.tlsUseDedicatedCertLabel: Use a dedicated cert for this route (opt out of the wildcard)
form.tlsUseDedicatedCertHelper: Use this for routes that need a separate key (e.g. payments, staging) — the route will request its own ACME cert alongside the wildcard.
form.healthCheckHelper: Leave a field blank to use the server default (GET / 30s / 5s / passes=1 / fails=1). URI is required.
form.upstreamPrivateIPHint: Private IP + https detected — the upstream certificate is probably self-signed. Use "Advanced upstream TLS" if the connection fails.
form.tlsAdvancedHelper: Tick only if the upstream presents a self-signed certificate (homelab Proxmox, Synology DSM, ESXi, UniFi). In production, prefer adding the CA to Arenet host's trust store.
form.wafModeStart: Start with Detect to spot false positives before enforcing.
form.uploadStreamingHelper: Disables WAF inspection of the request body AND Caddy's in-RAM buffering. Enable for large uploads where the body would otherwise be staged in RAM (Docker registry, file servers, video transcoders). Request headers + URL + response stay scanned. Leave this toggle OFF for API/web routes where the body is small and should be scanned.
form.wafDisableCRSLabel: Disable OWASP CRS rules (trusted internal APIs)
form.wafDisableCRSHelper: Stops loading the OWASP Core Rule Set on this route. No more SQLi / XSS / RCE / LFI / Protocol rules. The WAF handler stays wired (dashboard counter + audit log intact) but Coraza no longer loads any rule, so none can trigger. Use only for trusted internal APIs (LAN) or legacy routes where the CRS generates recurring false-positives. The WAF mode (off/detect/block) is preserved and effective the moment the CRS is re-enabled.
form.wafExcludeRulesHelper: Disables only the listed CRS rules on this route; the rest of the Core Rule Set stays active. Use when a specific rule fires a false-positive on legitimate traffic. 6-digit IDs in [200000, 999999], excluding the [100000, 199999] Arenet-reserved range. Comma or space separators.
form.wafExcludeRulesIdentifyRules: To identify rules that fired on this route recently, see
form.wafExcludeRulesIdentifyRulesGeneric: To identify rules that fired recently, see
form.wafExcludeRulesCRSDisabledWarning: ⚠️ The CRS is disabled via the toggle above — exclusions are silent until re-enabled.
form.wafExcludeTagsHelper: Disables every CRS rule carrying the listed tag on this route. More resilient than an ID list: a CRS update that adds rules to a tag automatically inherits the exclusion. Tag = a string with no spaces or commas inside (e.g. paranoia-level/3). Autocomplete suggests 24 common tags; any valid CRS tag is accepted.
form.rateLimitToggleLabelFull: Enable rate limiting (brute-force / API abuse protection)
form.rateLimitHelper: Beyond the maximum requests per period, requests are rejected with HTTP 429. Sliding-window algorithm (no hard reset at end of period). The default key {http.request.remote.host} resolves to the raw socket peer IP (no X-Forwarded-For trust — anti-spoof). On a deployment behind a trusted proxy, switch to {http.request.header.X-Forwarded-For} or similar.
form.errorPagesTemplateHelper: The Arenet branded default applies automatically to the 401 / 403 / 404 / 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504 codes if no template is attached.
form.errorPagesOverrideHelper: Overrides replace BOTH the template AND the default for the listed code. Leave blank to use the template or the default.
form.wafModeDetect: Detect — log matches, let traffic through
form.countryBlockSearchPlaceholder: Type FR, DE, Russia, United States…

dashboard.recentWafEmpty: No recent WAF events in the window.

users.subtitle: {total} account{plural_total} — {admins} admin{plural_admins}, {viewers} viewer{plural_viewers} · {oidc} OIDC, {local} local
users.toastCopyFailed: Copy failed — select the text manually
users.roleConfirmPromoteBody: {username} will get full admin access (CRUD on routes, settings and users).
users.roleConfirmDemoteBody: {username} will lose write access. Demoting the last local admin is blocked (break-glass invariant).
users.deleteConfirmServiceBody: {username} and its API token will be revoked immediately. This action is irreversible.
users.deleteConfirmLocalBody: {username} will be permanently deleted and all its sessions invalidated. Deleting the last local admin is blocked (break-glass invariant).
users.rotateModalIntro: The old token will be revoked immediately. All clients will not be able to authenticate until updated with the new token.
users.rotateRevealedShownOnce: New token shown only once. Copy it now.

oidc.emptyBody: No identity provider configured. The /users table only shows local accounts until SSO is enabled in Settings.
oidc.testScopesMissing: IdP reached ({ms} ms) but missing scopes: {scopes}
oidc.testOk: IdP reached in {ms} ms — scopes OK

channels.emptyBody: Add a first channel (webhook or email) to start receiving notifications. Channels are then targeted by rules in the Rules tab.
channels.severityTooltipPrefix: Minimum severity filtered by this channel. {tooltip}
channels.confirmMessage: Delete channel "{name}"? This action is irreversible.

rules.emptyBody: Add a first rule (WAF blocks threshold, certificate expiry, or system health) to trigger automatic notifications every 30 seconds. At least one channel must exist first in the Channels tab.
rules.summaryTestSkipped: Rule "{name}": {sent} sent, {skipped} skipped (channel disabled or below min severity).
rules.lastEvalTooltipPendingEnabled: Not yet evaluated — the watcher polls every 30 seconds; wait for the next tick.
rules.lastEvalTooltipDisabled: Rule disabled — the watcher ignores it.
rules.lastFireTooltipNever: Never fired. The condition has not been met since creation, or the rule is still in cooldown. Use the Test button to force a send without waiting.
rules.confirmMessage: Delete rule "{name}"? This action is irreversible.

history.degradedNotice: Observability unavailable: alert history is not accessible. Check that metrics.db is open.
history.emptyFiltered: No event matches the current filters.
history.emptyNoFilters: No event recorded yet. Create a channel then a rule in the tabs above, then click Test to generate a first event.

channelModal.typeLockedAfterCreate: The type cannot be changed after creation.
channelModal.redactedHeadersNote: Header values are redacted by the API. Leave the displayed value "[redacted]" as-is to keep the existing secret.
channelModal.bodyTemplatePlaceholders: Leave empty to send the full event as JSON. Placeholders:
channelModal.errWebhookUrlScheme: URL must start with http:// or https://.
channelModal.errTimeoutRange: Timeout must be between 1 and 60 seconds.
channelModal.errSmtpPortRange: SMTP port must be between 1 and 65535.
channelModal.errFromEmail: The "From" address must be a valid email.
channelModal.errAtLeastOneTo: At least one "To" recipient is required.

ruleModal.certExpiryHelper: Combined with a threshold (e.g. operator < and value 14) in the evaluation form below, alerts when a certificate is expiring soon.
ruleModal.certRenewalHelper: Combined with a threshold > 0 in the evaluation form below, alerts on any ACME renewal failure (Let's Encrypt, ZeroSSL…) within the window. Default 24h — matches Let's Encrypt's retry cadence.
ruleModal.kindLockedAfterCreate: The type cannot be changed after creation.
ruleModal.channelsEmpty: No active channel available. Create one first in the Channels tab.
ruleModal.errNameSlug: Name must be lowercase, alphanumeric and dashes only (1-64 characters).
ruleModal.errCooldownRange: Cooldown must be between 30 seconds and 24 hours.
ruleModal.errThresholdNumeric: The threshold value must be numeric.
ruleModal.errExpectedEmpty: The expected value cannot be empty.
ruleModal.errWafWindowRange: The WAF window must be between 60 seconds and 24 hours.
ruleModal.errCertRenewalWindowRange: The renewal failure window must be between 60 seconds and 7 days.

audit.subtitle: Review authentication events and route mutations.
audit.emptyFiltered: No audit events match the current filters.

setup.subtitle: Create the initial administrator account.
setup.infoBannerBody: Look in the Arenet server logs for a line starting with
setup.infoBannerBodySuffix: The token is regenerated on every restart while no administrator exists.
setup.errAdminExists: Setup unavailable: an administrator account already exists.

errorPages.subtitle: HTML templates served by Caddy for status codes 401/403/404/429/500/502/503/504. With no template attached to a route, the Arenet branded default applies automatically.
errorPages.emptyBody: All routes currently receive the Arenet branded default. Create a template to customise the error pages of one or several routes.
errorPages.btnDuplicateBuiltinTooltip: Create a new template based on the default
errorPages.btnDuplicateTooltip: Create a new template based on this one
errorPages.readOnlyBannerBody: — this template is the Arenet default. Click "Duplicate to customise" in the top right to create an editable copy.
errorPages.catchallHint: This template's 404 page will be served for any request reaching a host not configured in Arenet. Only one template can be catch-all at a time.
errorPages.varsHelp: These placeholders are substituted by Caddy at request time (not in the preview, which uses fake values).
errorPages.deleteModalBody: Deleting "{name}" is irreversible. Routes that reference it will revert to the Arenet branded default.
errorPages.toastReadOnly: The "Arenet default" template is read-only; use "Duplicate".

settingsSubcards.automationResetHelper: To disable the auto-classifier cleanly, use Reset (bottom left) — the BoltDB configuration is wiped and the LAPI writer is detached immediately (audit row automation_reset). The trigger rules below are not touched: you can keep your config for future re-activation.
settingsSubcards.automationResetDialogMessage: The auto-classifier will stop pushing decisions to LAPI immediately. The trigger rules are not touched — you can keep them for future re-activation. No impact on the CrowdSec bouncer configuration (read-side).
settingsSubcards.revokeSessionMessage: The other device will be signed out immediately.

oidcSettings.title: OIDC SSO
oidcSettings.subtitle: Delegate admin login to an external identity provider. Local login remains available regardless (break-glass).
oidcSettings.providerHelper: Choose your OIDC provider to display its logo on the sign-in button. Leave empty to use the generic logo. The choice does NOT influence the authentication logic — it is purely visual.
oidcSettings.issuerHelper: OIDC issuer URL (the issuer field of the discovery document). DO NOT include the /.well-known/openid-configuration suffix — Arenet appends it automatically (if you paste the full URL, the suffix is silently stripped server-side). Authentik: /application/o/<slug>/ ; Keycloak: /realms/<realm> ; Authelia: the deployment root.
oidcSettings.scopesHelper: Space- or comma-separated. openid is mandatory; email is required for the canonicalisation step.
oidcSettings.labelAcceptUnverifiedEmail: Accept unverified email on first login (advanced)
oidcSettings.acceptUnverifiedEmailHelper: Off by default. Enable only when your IdP does not assert email_verified=true on its ID tokens (Authentik admin-created accounts being the typical case) and you fully control IdP-side account creation. Relaxes the Δ7 guard for the allowlist Email-bootstrap pass — only safe when the IdP itself is trusted.
oidcSettings.allowlistSubtitle: Only emails on this list can log in via SSO. New entries are "pending" until the user's first login canonicalises them.
oidcSettings.allowlistAddHelper: To find the OIDC Subject ID, look it up in your IdP's admin console. Leaving this field empty uses email-bootstrap on first login (requires the IdP to emit email_verified=true).

crowdsecSettings.title: CrowdSec bouncer
crowdsecSettings.subtitle: IP-reputation gate. Reads block decisions from the LAPI and rejects connections from sources CrowdSec flagged. Bouncer runs in-process; no extra container needed if CrowdSec engine is already on this host.
crowdsecSettings.lapiUrlHelper: LAPI listen URL. http://127.0.0.1:8080 for an apt install on the same host; http://crowdsec:8080 for a sibling Docker container in the same compose network; or any custom URL the deployment exposes.
crowdsecSettings.apiKeyHelper: Generate with cscli bouncers add arenet on the CrowdSec host. The key is shown only once at creation; if lost, delete + re-add the bouncer.
crowdsecSettings.bouncerNameHelper: Cosmetic identifier. Must match the name used in cscli bouncers add.
crowdsecSettings.timeoutHelper: Per-request cap on LAPI calls. Range 1–60. Default 5.
crowdsecSettings.saveFooter: Save & apply hot-reloads the embedded Caddy without a process restart. Active routes drop the request only after the new bouncer creds are live, so there is no race window. To disable the bouncer cleanly, use Reset (bottom left) — the BoltDB configuration is wiped and the bouncer drops out of the data plane immediately (audit row crowdsec_reset).
crowdsecSettings.resetDialogMessage: The bouncer will stop gating traffic immediately. Routes remain protected by the WAF + rate-limiter, but the IP-reputation gate will be disabled. No impact on the Security Automation configuration (watcher credentials).

backupSection.title: Backup & restore
backupSection.subtitle: Export the entire Arenet configuration as JSON, or restore from a previously-exported file.
backupSection.exportHelper: Default export redacts secrets (admin password hashes, OVH keys, OIDC client secret, forward-auth client secrets, per-route Basic Auth hashes). Use "Include secrets" only for disaster-recovery archives that you can store securely.
backupSection.restoreHelper: Choose a previously-exported file. The restore is all-or-nothing: any validation failure aborts before any change is written to storage.
backupSection.allowIncompleteHelper: Sentinels that cannot inherit from this instance will be cleared. Affected secrets need to be re-saved.
backupSection.allowEmptyUsersHelper: Accept a backup with zero users; the next boot will re-trigger the setup-token flow.
backupSection.confirmDialogMessage: The exported file will contain plaintext admin password hashes, OVH API keys, OIDC client secret, forward-auth client secrets, and per-route Basic Auth hashes. The browser will save the file with its default permissions — store it to a restricted location and consider encrypting at rest (age / GPG / vault).

serverPosition.title: Server position
serverPosition.subtitle: Center of the threat map (page /map). Detected automatically at startup via ipify + GeoLite2, or set manually below.
serverPosition.degradedBody: Automatic detection did not find the GeoLite2-City database. Place it at /var/lib/arenet/GeoLite2-City.mmdb (or set the ARENET_GEOIP_MMDB env var), then click "Re-detect" — or enter a manual position below.
serverPosition.toastDetectFailed: Detection failed: MMDB missing or ipify offline. Degraded state.

crowdsecDecisions.panelSubtitle: Arenet-side snapshot of the decisions received from LAPI.
crowdsecDecisions.mirrorNotConfiguredBody: The CrowdSec bouncer on the Caddy side and the StreamBouncer consumer on the Arenet side share the same LAPI key.
crowdsecDecisions.emptyActive: No active decision. The bouncer is not blocking any source currently.
crowdsecDecisions.emptyWindow: No decision in the window. The bouncer has never received a decision from LAPI.
crowdsecDecisions.liveNotConfiguredBody: The bouncer is not configured. Go to
crowdsecDecisions.liveNotConfiguredSuffix: to enter the LAPI URL and the bouncer key.
crowdsecDecisions.liveStaleNotice: Showing data from: last successful polling (the table
crowdsecDecisions.liveStaleNoticeSuffix: may be slightly out of date)
crowdsecDecisions.emptyLiveAll: No active decision according to LAPI. The bouncer is watching but no source is currently blocked. (CAPI sync runs every ~2-15 min; a freshly-started bouncer may take a few minutes to receive the community blocklist.)
crowdsecDecisions.emptyLiveFiltered: No decision in this category ({total} total — see the {tabAll} tab).
crowdsecDecisions.scenariosSubtitle: CrowdSec scenarios that fired over the last
crowdsecDecisions.automationNotConfiguredFooter: independently — this coupling only concerns the tab
crowdsecDecisions.scenariosEmptyHelp: Either no attack detected, or Arenet logs not yet

createServiceAccount.revealedSuffix: . Copy it now — it will never be retrievable again. Rotation generates a new token.
createServiceAccount.toastCopyFailed: Copy failed — select the text manually

banIp.title: Ban an IP
banIp.maskWarn: ⚠ You are about to ban {count} IPs.
banIp.reasonPlaceholder: Why this ban? (visible in the audit log)
banIp.notConfiguredBody: Manual ban uses the watcher credentials from Security Automation. Go to
banIp.notConfiguredSuffix: and enter your watcher ({cmd} on the CrowdSec host).

certs.pageSubtitle: ACME-managed TLS certificates. Wildcard apex configuration ships under managed domains; per-route certs are auto-provisioned by certmagic when TLS is enabled.
certs.renewalHelper: All certificates are automatically renewed by certmagic (Caddy v2) ~30 days before expiry, with exponential retry on failure. No manual action required. Renewal logs are available on the {logsLink} page.
certs.certsLoadError: Could not fetch certificates (backend returned an error). The rest of this page remains usable.
certs.certsEmptyHelper: Certificates are auto-provisioned when a TLS route is created or when a managed apex is declared.
certs.staleBadgeTooltip: Last failure: {ago} · {count} attempt{plural} without successful renewal. Click to view history.
certs.policiesLead: A managed apex issues ONE wildcard certificate via DNS-01 (covers every subdomain route). The DNS provider is configured under {settingsLink}.
certs.dnsUnconfiguredHelper: Wildcard issuance is disabled — covered routes will serve self-signed certs from Caddy's internal CA until you configure the DNS provider in
certs.policiesFoot: Declaring an apex marks every existing route under *.<apex> as covered by the wildcard. The per-route ACME selector is hidden in the route editor, and the certificate is provisioned once for every subdomain.
certs.mdDeleteLead: Covered routes' ACMEChallenge will be reverted. Pick the post-revert challenge value below.
certs.mdDeleteWarning: Heads up. Each covered route will request its own HTTP-01 cert on the next reload. Many routes on one apex may hit Let's Encrypt's per-domain rate limit (50 certs / week).
certs.drillEmpty: No lifecycle event observed for this domain in the retention window (90 days).
certs.drillLead: The {count} most recent event{plural} for this domain, newest first.
certs.wizardApexHint: Bare domain (no leading *.) — the wildcard is implied. Issues a cert for {wildcard}.
certs.wizardIncludeApexLabel: Include bare apex in cert SAN

emptyState.ctaLabel: Configure a DNS provider in Settings

waf.pageSubtitle: The Coraza engine applies the OWASP Core Rule Set. Event counts shown below reflect blocked requests over the last 24h, per category.
waf.disabledHelper: WAF event counts are surfaced via the observability subsystem, which failed to start. Coraza is still inspecting traffic; only the dashboard projection is missing.
waf.categoriesReadOnlyNotice: Granular category control (enable/disable per category) is deferred to a future step. Counts shown below are read-only.
waf.drillEmpty: No rule triggered in this category in the last 24h.
waf.ipEnforcementHelper: The full timeline of blocked IPs (auto + manual). Manual list management is deferred (see backlog).

topology.title: Topology
topology.lede: Reverse-proxy entry points on the left, upstream services on the right. The thickness and brightness of each line reflect the real-time throughput of that flow.

sidebar.legendNote: Thickness is computed on the 60-second rolling average. The animation points in the dominant traffic direction — incoming requests towards the services.

logs.pageSubtitle: Real-time stream of WAF events, throttling, authentication failures and certificate lifecycle. Full 2xx/3xx traffic capture is deferred — see backlog.
logs.emptyNoEvents: No events in the current window.
